content: Just as the linguistic concept of reflexion requires learners to internalize new vocabulary by connecting it to their own prior lived experiences to deepen comprehension, current LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be improved by grounding abstract internal debugging in this pedagogical model of reflective vocabulary learning. LLM self-reflection can be made more robust by prompting agents to map flawed outputs to their own stored memory of prior correct and incorrect generations, much like language learners deepen mastery through explicit reflective practice linking new words to their own knowledge.
